supply_chain.dependencies: Principal raw materials include triexta, nylon, polypropylene, polyester resins and fibers (carpet business); clay, talc, feldspar, glazes (ceramic tile business); wood, paper, resins (laminate/wood flooring); glass fiber, plasticizers, PVC resins (resilient flooring). Company is dependent on one or a small number of suppliers for certain raw materials.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruption from geopolitical conflict (Russia-Ukraine, Middle East conflicts), tariffs and global trade uncertainties, raw material sourcing from Ukraine (primarily clay), potential escalation of oil and petroleum-based chemical prices, transportation barriers, supplier relationship disruptions

regulatory_risks: Increasingly numerous and complex laws and regulations in jurisdictions where company operates

Enhanced climate change and environmental regulation (California Climate Corporate Data Accountability Act, EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive, EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive)
Greenhouse gas emissions limitations and cap-and-trade systems
Federal, state, local and international environmental, social, health and safety laws
Compliance with laws governing international relations and trade, including US and EU sanctions and corruption laws
Foreign country regulatory requirements and differing labor laws
Tax law changes and foreign country tax rules
Data privacy and cybersecurity regulations (federal, state, international)
Trade and tariff policies, including potential increased US tariffs on foreign imports from Mexico, China or other countries

sanction_risks: US and EU sanctions on Russia due to military actions in Ukraine
Potential nationalization of foreign-owned businesses in Russia
Sanctions or transportation barriers from Middle East conflicts
Compliance with US and European Union laws relating to sanctions and corruption in emerging markets
